sections.name: Gobmob Snake
sections.text: These bizarre mutant goblin snakes possess no fewer than three heads.

sections.name: Goblin Snake
Indolent Overlords
sections.text: The exact nature of the relationship between goblins and goblin snakes is a matter of some contention, with some goblins viewing the creatures as minor demigods or the ascended souls of fallen heroes, and others viewing them as abominations to be destroyed on sight. A goblin snake fortunate enough to find a goblin community of the former sort usually treats its adoring followers as inferiors, if not outright servants, and expects them to ply it with regular offerings of food and valuables.
A goblin snake is a foul-smelling serpentine creature covered in greasy black scales and sporting a fanged, lipless head resembling that of a goblin.

traits.name: Uncommon
traits.text: Something of uncommon rarity requires special training or comes from a particular culture or part of the world. Some character choices give access to uncommon options, and the GM can choose to allow access for anyone. Less is known about uncommon creatures than common creatures. They typically can't be summoned. The DC of Recall Knowledge checks related to these creature is increased by 2.

traits.name: Aberration
traits.text: Aberrations are creatures from beyond the planes or corruptions of the natural order.

traits.name: Goblin
traits.text: A creature with this trait is a small, frantic goblinoid. Goblins tend to have darkvision. An ability with this trait can be used or chosen only by goblins. A weapon with this trait is created and used by goblins.

traits.name: Mutant
traits.text: The monster has mutated or evolved, granting it unusual benefits, drawbacks, or both.

interaction_abilities.name: Snake Empathy

traits.name: Primal
traits.text:
This magic comes from the primal tradition, connecting to the natural world and instinct. Anything with this trait is magical.  
  
A creature with this trait is primarily constituted of or has a strong connection to primal magic.

interaction_abilities.text: A gobmob snake can communicate with snakes.

automatic_abilities.name: Incessant Yammering

traits.name: Auditory
traits.text: Auditory actions and effects rely on sound. An action with the auditory trait can be successfully performed only if the creature using the action can speak or otherwise produce the required sounds. A spell or effect with the auditory trait has its effect only if the target can hear it. This applies only to sound-based parts of the effect, as determined by the GM. This is different from a sonic effect, which still affects targets who can’t hear it (such as deaf targets) as long as the effect itself makes sound.

traits.name: Aura
traits.text: An aura is an emanation that continually ebbs out from you, affecting creatures within a certain radius. Aura can also refer to the magical signature of an item.

traits.name: Linguistic
traits.text: An effect with this trait depends on language comprehension. A linguistic effect that targets a creature works only if the target understands the language you are using.

automatic_abilities.text: A gobmob snake's heads constantly bicker and snipe at one another, annoying and distracting anyone nearby. Each non-goblin creature that begins its turn in the aura must attempt a DC 21 Will save. On a failure, it takes a –1 status penalty to Perception checks and Will saves for 1 round. On a success, it is temporarily immune for 1 minute.

automatic_abilities.name: Infighting
automatic_abilities.text: Whenever a gobmob snake critically fails at an attack roll or skill check, it must succeed at a DC 5 flat check or become slowed 1 as its heads argue over which of them is to blame. An enemy can provoke an argument by attempting a DC 20 Deception check as a single action with the auditory, concentrate, linguistic, and mental traits.

automatic_abilities.name: Coiled Strike
automatic_abilities.text: As Reactive Strike, but the gobmob snake can use this reaction only if it's Coiled.

traits.name: Finesse
traits.text: You can use your Dexterity modifier instead of your Strength modifier on attack rolls using this melee weapon. You still calculate damage using Strength.

traits.name: Unarmed
traits.text: An unarmed attack uses your body rather than a manufactured weapon. An unarmed attack isn’t a weapon, though has a weapon group and might have weapon traits. An unarmed attack can’t be Disarmed. It also doesn’t take up a hand, though a fist or other grasping appendage generally works like a free-hand weapon.

ability.name: Coil
ability.text: The gobmob snake uses an action to coil itself, increasing its reach with its fangs from 5 to 10 feet. After the goblin snake Strikes with its fangs, it becomes uncoiled.

ability.name: Goblin Breath

traits.name: Olfactory
traits.text: An olfactory effect can affect only creatures that can smell it. This applies only to olfactory parts of the effect, as determined by the GM.

traits.name: Poison
traits.text: An effect with this trait delivers a poison or deals poison damage. An item with this trait is poisonous and might cause an affliction.

ability.text: The gobmob snake belches a cloud of nauseating vapor in a 15-foot cone. Non-goblin creatures within the cloud must succeed at a DC 20 Fortitude save or become sickened 1. On a critical failure, a creature is also slowed 1 for as long as it is sickened. Creatures that successfully save are immune for 24 hours. The goblin snake can't use Goblin Breath again for 1d4 rounds.
